content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: We both need src/click/shell_completion.py:_resolve_context (lines ~547,560,571) — real conflict, let's avoid double-editing. Proposal: you own shell_completion.py entirely (your leak-fix wrapping with 'with' statements). I'll own core.py entirely (Context.validate_nesting param + child tracking + close()/__exit__ validation + _make_sub_context inheritance). Since my spec also requires shell_completion.py to pass validate_nesting=False when creating contexts during completion, can you add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to the 3 make_context(...) calls in _resolve_context (lines ~547, 560, 571-578) as part of your edit? That's a trivial one-kwarg addition per call site, and it means only one of us touches that file. Let me know if that works — then I'll start on core.py.

type: ACCEPT

your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Agreed. I'll only add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to the make_context(...) calls in _resolve_context (no control-flow changes) and own all of core.py (Context.__init__ param, child tracking, close()/__exit__ validation, _make_sub_context). You own _resolve_context's control-flow (with-wrapping) and won't touch Context.__init__. We'll merge via git merge and resolve any conflicts in _resolve_context by hand since they should be small non-overlapping line changes. Starting implementation now.
